frameworks.name: reference-dependent valuation (behavioral economics)
frameworks.one_line: Reference point conditions whether outcomes feel positive or negative.

frameworks.name: deep brain stimulation of anterior cingulate cortex
frameworks.one_line: Prior DBS depression treatment reinterpreted as reference-point modulation.

core_question: Is the decisional reference point pathologically altered in major depressive disorder, and can it serve as a diagnostic marker?
key_assumptions: Reference point is the central mechanism conditioning reinforcer evaluation
Reference point setting is a measurable dynamic process
Anterior cingulate cortex implements reference-point computation
manipulations: Reinforcer events used to probe reference-point evaluation and setting dynamics
key_findings: Reference point is significantly and pathologically elevated in depressed patients
Reference-point setting dynamics are profoundly impaired in depression
interpretations: Reference-point pathology is a novel mechanism of MDD, linking ACC DBS efficacy to reference-point modulation
Findings support a brief virtual diagnostic test for depression
